ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, suggesting no significant depressive symptoms. Children and adolescents commonly experience some mood fluctuations, and your current emotional functioning appears healthy.
suggestions: Continue encouraging open communication about feelings
Maintain regular routines including consistent sleep schedules
Support engagement in enjoyable activities and social interactions
Occasional sadness is a normal part of development

ranges.label: Mild Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depressive symptoms. At this level, a young person may show some changes in mood, energy, or interest in activities. These symptoms are worth monitoring but may not yet significantly impair daily functioning.
suggestions: Monitor mood changes over the coming weeks to see if they persist or worsen
Encourage regular physical activity, social engagement, and consistent sleep
Create a supportive environment where the young person feels safe expressing emotions
Consider speaking with a school counselor or pediatrician if concerns continue
Limit excessive screen time and encourage meaningful activities

ranges.label: Significant Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates significant depressive symptoms that are likely affecting daily functioning, school performance, and social relationships. At this level, professional evaluation is recommended to determine appropriate support and intervention.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional experienced with youth
Talk to the young person's pediatrician or school counselor about available resources
Maintain a structured, supportive daily routine at home
Evidence-based treatments such as CBT or interpersonal therapy are effective for youth depression
If there are any concerns about self-harm, seek immediate professional help

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, indicating no clinically significant depressive symptoms. Your emotional functioning appears healthy, and you are coping well with everyday challenges.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle with regular exercise and balanced nutrition
Keep up regular social activities and meaningful relationships
Practice good sleep hygiene and stress management techniques

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depressive symptoms that may be affecting your daily life. You may notice changes in sleep patterns, appetite, energy levels, or a general sense of not feeling like yourself. These symptoms are worth monitoring.
suggestions: Pay attention to changes in your mood and energy levels over the coming weeks
Maintain regular exercise and social engagement, which can help improve mood
Try relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, meditation, or progressive muscle relaxation
Consider keeping a mood journal to track patterns and triggers
Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist for more than two weeks

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that may significantly affect your daily functioning, work performance, and relationships. You may experience persistent low mood, difficulty concentrating, reduced motivation, or physical symptoms like fatigue and sleep disturbance.
suggestions: Consult a mental health professional for a thorough evaluation
Consider evidence-based psychotherapy such as cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
Maintain a structured daily routine to provide stability and purpose
Avoid alcohol and other substances that may worsen depressive symptoms
Stay connected with supportive people; avoid isolating yourself

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depressive symptoms that are likely significantly impairing your daily functioning, relationships, and overall quality of life. Professional help is strongly recommended at this level, as effective treatments are available.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a psychiatrist or mental health professional as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not stay alone; inform a trusted family member or friend about your situation
Professional treatment including medication and therapy is essential at this level
Focus on basic self-care: adequate sleep, nutrition, and gentle physical activity

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: Your score suggests low risk for perinatal depression. Your emotional adjustment during the perinatal period appears to be within a healthy range. Mood fluctuations are common during pregnancy and postpartum, and your current level of functioning appears good.
suggestions: Continue monitoring your mood as hormonal and life changes continue
Maintain supportive relationships and accept help when offered
Ensure adequate rest, nutrition, and physical activity appropriate for your stage
Baby blues (mild mood changes in the first two weeks postpartum) are normal and usually resolve on their own

ranges.label: Possible Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates possible depressive symptoms that warrant monitoring. During the perinatal period, hormonal changes, sleep deprivation, and the major life transition of parenthood can contribute to emotional difficulties. Early attention to these symptoms can prevent worsening.
suggestions: Talk to your obstetrician, midwife, or primary care provider about how you are feeling
Ensure you have adequate social support and practical help with childcare
Prioritize rest whenever possible; sleep deprivation significantly affects mood
Consider joining a new parent support group for shared experiences and encouragement
Rescreen in 2-4 weeks to monitor whether symptoms improve or worsen

ranges.label: Probable Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests probable perinatal depression that warrants professional evaluation. These symptoms may be affecting your ability to care for yourself and your baby, your relationship with your partner, and your overall well-being. Perinatal depression is common and highly treatable.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a healthcare provider experienced in perinatal mental health
Evidence-based treatments such as CBT and interpersonal therapy are effective and safe during the perinatal period
Do not hesitate to accept help with childcare and household tasks
Some antidepressant medications are considered safe during pregnancy and breastfeeding; discuss options with your doctor
Remember that seeking help is a sign of strength, not weakness, and benefits both you and your baby

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe depressive symptoms during the perinatal period that require immediate professional attention. At this level, symptoms are likely significantly impairing your functioning and may affect bonding with your baby. Effective treatments are available and important to pursue promptly.
suggestions: Contact your healthcare provider or a perinatal mental health specialist as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of harming yourself or your baby, call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline or Postpartum Support International: 1-800-944-4773)
Do not leave yourself alone if you are feeling unsafe; ask a family member or friend to stay with you
Comprehensive treatment including therapy and/or medication is essential
With proper treatment, perinatal depression resolves in the vast majority of cases

ranges.label: Minimal Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score suggests minimal depressive symptoms. Your emotional functioning appears healthy, and you are coping well with everyday stressors. The CES-D-10 is a brief screening tool, and this result is reassuring.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy habits that support emotional well-being
Keep up regular exercise, social activities, and balanced nutrition
Practice stress prevention through adequate sleep and relaxation techniques

ranges.label: Mild Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild depressive symptoms that are worth monitoring. You may be experiencing some emotional discomfort, changes in sleep or energy, or reduced enjoyment of daily activities.
suggestions: Monitor your mood over the next few weeks to see if symptoms persist
Maintain regular routines for sleep, exercise, and social engagement
Practice self-care activities that you find restorative and enjoyable
Consider talking to a trusted person about how you are feeling

ranges.label: Moderate Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that may be affecting your daily functioning. A score of 10 or above on the CES-D-10 is commonly used as a screening threshold for clinically significant depression, and further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for a comprehensive evaluation
Maintain regular physical activity, which has strong evidence for reducing depression
Stay connected with supportive people and avoid social withdrawal
Consider evidence-based approaches such as cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
A structured daily routine can help provide stability during difficult periods

ranges.label: Severe Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depressive symptoms that are likely significantly impacting your daily functioning, relationships, and quality of life. This score strongly suggests the need for professional evaluation and intervention.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a mental health professional as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Inform a trusted family member or friend about how you are feeling
Professional treatment including therapy and/or medication can be very effective
Focus on basic self-care: adequate sleep, nutrition, and gentle activity
